strategies.name: FMA with NaN and Inf
strategies.target: fma instruction with NaN and Inf operands
strategies.rationale: The patch infers fast-math flags for fma based on the known classes of its operands. If the operands are known to be NaN or Inf, the inferred flags might be incorrect, leading to incorrect simplifications.
strategies.expected_issue: The fma instruction might be incorrectly simplified or have incorrect fast-math flags inferred, leading to incorrect results or undefined behavior.

strategies.name: FMA with Subnormal Operands
strategies.rationale: The patch infers fast-math flags for fma based on the known classes of its operands. If the operands are subnormal, the inferred flags might be incorrect, leading to incorrect simplifications.
strategies.expected_issue: The fma instruction might be incorrectly simplified or have incorrect fast-math flags inferred, leading to incorrect results or undefined behavior.

strategies.name: FMA with Zero Operands
strategies.rationale: The patch infers fast-math flags for fma based on the known classes of its operands. If the operands are zero, the inferred flags might be incorrect, leading to incorrect simplifications.
strategies.expected_issue: The fma instruction might be incorrectly simplified or have incorrect fast-math flags inferred, leading to incorrect results or undefined behavior.

strategies.name: FMA with Negative Operands
strategies.rationale: The patch infers fast-math flags for fma based on the known classes of its operands. If the operands are negative, the inferred flags might be incorrect, leading to incorrect simplifications.
strategies.expected_issue: The fma instruction might be incorrectly simplified or have incorrect fast-math flags inferred, leading to incorrect results or undefined behavior.

strategies.name: FMA with Mixed Operands
strategies.rationale: The patch infers fast-math flags for fma based on the known classes of its operands. If the operands are a mix of NaN, Inf, subnormal, zero, and negative values, the inferred flags might be incorrect, leading to incorrect simplifications.
strategies.expected_issue: The fma instruction might be incorrectly simplified or have incorrect fast-math flags inferred, leading to incorrect results or undefined behavior.

define half @ret_only_inf(half %x, half %y, half %z) {
  %result = call half @llvm.fma.f16(half %x, half %y, half %z)
  %cmp = fcmp uno half %result, 0.0
  %ret = select i1 %cmp, half 0xH7C00, half %result
  ret half %ret
}

declare half @llvm.fma.f16(half, half, half)

define half @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call half @ret_only_inf(half 0xH7C00, half 0xH0000, half 0xH0000)
  ret half %r
}

declare nofpclass(inf zero sub norm) half @returns_nan()

define nofpclass(nan) half @ret_nonan__select_nan_or_unknown_src1(i1 %cond, half %unknown0, half %unknown1, half %unknown2) {
  %nan = call half @returns_nan()
  %nan.or.unknown = select i1 %cond, half %nan, half %unknown1
  %result = call half @llvm.fma.f16(half %unknown0, half %nan.or.unknown, half %unknown2)
  ret half %result
}

declare half @llvm.fma.f16(half, half, half)

define half @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call half @ret_nonan__select_nan_or_unknown_src1(i1 true, half 0xH7C00, half 0xH7C00, half 0xH7C00)
  ret half %r
}
